key_insight: DeFAb turns defeasible abduction into a verifiable benchmark where symbolic checks judge whether a hypothesis correctly explains anomalies without breaking unrelated defaults.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark provides a high-precision testbed for evaluating and training foundation models on theory revision, with exact rewards that can support DPO, RLVR, and GRPO-style optimization.
method: Converts public knowledge bases into defeasible abduction instances with polynomial-time verifiers for derivation, conservativity, and minimality.
Combines taxonomic and behavioral graph sources to generate 372,648+ instances across three difficulty levels.
Adds harder variants, including DeFAb-Hard and CONJURE, to stress-test reasoning beyond surface-form matching.
evidence_signal: A rule-based solver achieves 100% accuracy in under 50 microseconds, while frontier language models peak at 65% and fall to 23.5% under rendering-robust evaluation.
watch_item: Performance appears highly sensitive to surface rendering, so benchmark results may overstate robustness unless evaluation covers multiple textual formats and contamination is carefully controlled.
paper_title: DeFAb: A Verifiable Benchmark for Defeasible Abduction in Foundation Models
